does: This paper conducts an empirical study of prompt injection and goal hijacking attacks against GPT-3 and other contemporary LLMs in 2022, establishing baseline vulnerability metrics showing 31% attack success rates. The core contribution is proposing Adversarial Fine-Tuning as a defense mechanism, where models are fine-tuned on adversarial examples to improve robustness. The paper evaluates this approach across GPT-3 variants (Ada, Babbage, Curie, Davinci) and demonstrates that adversarial fine-tuning can reduce attack success rates to near-zero on smaller models, while revealing that larger, more capable models like Davinci remain significantly more vulnerable than their smaller counterparts.
connects: This work builds on emerging concerns about prompt injection vulnerabilities in LLMs that gained prominence around 2022 as large language models became widely deployed in production systems. It engages with foundational ideas from adversarial machine learning and adversarial training, adapting these established defensive techniques to the new problem domain of natural language prompt attacks. The paper responds to early recognized risks in instruction-following models and connects to parallel research on jailbreaking and misuse of LLMs, establishing prompt injection as a distinct attack vector requiring systematic study alongside broader adversarial robustness concerns in neural language models.
enables: This early documentation establishes a historical baseline for understanding how prompt injection defenses have evolved, providing reference points for measuring progress in this critical security domain. By identifying limitations of fine-tuning-based approaches and revealing the vulnerability gradient across model scales, the work points toward future research directions including developing more robust defense mechanisms that work effectively at scale, understanding fundamental tradeoffs between model capability and prompt robustness, and exploring alternative defense paradigms beyond fine-tuning. The findings underscore the necessity for security-focused research as LLM deployment accelerates and motivate investigation into defenses that maintain both safety and performance across the full range of model sizes.
